title: 10 best herbal teas for palpitation
intro: The top herbal teas for palpitation include chamomile, lavender, and valerian root. Chamomile is a gentle relaxant that soothes the nervous system, reducing heart rate and irregular heart rhythms. Lavender boasts calming properties that promote relaxation and ease anxiety, which often triggers heart palpitations. Valerian root is a powerful herb known for its sedative effects, helping to regulate heartbeat and promote sound sleep. These three herbs, when consumed as teas, offer a natural and effective solution to alleviate palpitations and support cardiovascular health.

teas.tea_desc: Passionflower tea contains harmala alkaloids, which possess natural sedative and anti-arrhythmic properties. These compounds help to regulate heart function, reducing irregular heartbeats or palpitations. Passionflower also contains flavonoids, which relax smooth muscles, further contributing to its ability to alleviate heart palpitations. Additionally, it inhibits the breakdown of a neurotransmitter called GABA, promoting calmness and regulating heart rhythms. Passionflower tea's soothing effects on the nervous system also help to decrease anxiety and stress, often triggers of heart palpitations.
tea_parts: Leaves: Passionflower tea made from the leaves is known to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate heart rate and alleviate palpitations.

Flowers: The fragrant flowers of the passionflower plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help soothe symptoms of palpitations.
Stems: Passionflower stems are rich in alkaloids and flavonoids that have been shown to have a sedative effect, helping to reduce heart rate and palpitations.

Bark: The bark of the passionflower plant contains various beneficial compounds, including alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which can help regulate heart function and alleviate symptoms of palpitations.
Roots: Passionflower roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat heart-related conditions, including palpitations, due to their sedative and calming effects.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants help reduce anxiety and stress, which can lead to palpitations.
Alkaloids: Certain alkaloids in passionflower, like harman and harmaline, possess sedative and muscle relaxant properties that can help regulate heartbeat.
Chrysin: This flavonoid contributes to the anti-anxiety and mild sedative effects, which can help manage symptoms of palpitations.
Benzoflavone: This compound displays cardioprotective activities, which can help maintain a regular heart rhythm.
Vitexin: This flavonoid exhibits vasodilatory effects, promoting healthy blood flow and heart function.
Orientin: Acting as an antioxidant, it helps protect heart tissues from oxidative damage, important for those experiencing palpitations.
Isoorientin: Similar to orientin, it also have antioxidant properties, which contribute to overall heart health and regular heart function.
Schaftoside: This flavonoid has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, thus aiding in the control of heart palpitations.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water, then pour it over 1 tablespoon of dried passionflower leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes to extract the plant's compounds.
Strain the liquid to remove the spent passionflower leaves.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the passionflower tea while warm, up to three times a day.

teas.tea_desc: Lavender tea contains antioxidants and volatile compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which possess soothing properties. These elements work together to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and decrease heart rate, all of which alleviate palpitations. Moreover, lavender's anxiolytic properties help stabilize the nervous system, thereby preventing irregular heart rhythms. Additionally, lavender tea's calming effects on the mind and body help lower blood pressure, consequently minimizing palpitations. Lastly, lavender's mild sedative nature encourages improved sleep quality, indirectly contributing to a stable heart rate and reduced palpitations.
tea_parts: Flowers: Lavender tea made from the flowers is known to have calming effects, reducing palpitations caused by anxiety or stress.
Leaves: The leaves of lavender tea plant contain calming compounds that help regulate heartbeat, reducing palpitations.
Buds: Lavender tea made from buds is rich in soothing agents that promote relaxation, providing relief from palpitations.
Stems: The stems of lavender tea plant contain calming compounds that help control palpitations caused by high blood pressure.
tea_constituents: Linalool: A terpene alcohol recognized for its ability to reduce anxiety and heart rate, helping to alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
Acetate: An ester that possesses anti-inflammatory and calming properties, promoting normal heart function.
Eucalyptol: A terpene known for its soothing effects on the nervous system, reducing palpitations caused by anxiety.
Camphor: A compound with therapeutic properties that regulate heart rhythm and alleviate heart palpitations.
Cineole: A compound that exhibits anti-arrhythmic and heart rate-stabilizing properties, preventing irregular heartbeats.
Geraniol: A terpene alcohol known for its relaxant effects on the heart muscles, reducing instances of palpitation.
Limonene: A terpene responsible for stress reduction and heart rate normalization, beneficial for treating palpitations.
tea_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried lavender flowers to a tea infuser or teapot for 7-10 minutes.
Pour the boiling water over the lavender flowers and let it steep for 4-5 minutes.
Remove the lavender flowers from the tea by straining or lifting the infuser.
Sip the lavender tea slowly to alleviate symptoms of palpitation.

teas.tea_desc: Chamomile tea contains antioxidants and flavonoids, such as apigenin, that bind to benzodiazepine receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. These compounds have a mild sedative effect, slowing heart rate and decreasing blood pressure. Chamomile tea also possesses anti-inflammatory properties, which can dilate blood vessels and improve overall circulation. Furthermore, it inhibits the release of histamine, a compound involved in allergic reactions that can trigger heart palpitations. By consuming chamomile tea regularly, individuals can experience a consistent decrease in palpitations due to its combined anxiolytic, sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine effects.
tea_parts: Flowers: Chamomile tea is primarily made using the flowers of the plant, which contain calming properties that help soothe the nervous system, promoting normal heart rhythm and reducing palpitations.

Leaves: The leaves of the chamomile plant add to the overall therapeutic effect of the tea, providing essential oils and antioxidants that can further aid in the relaxation of the heart muscles and the prevention of irregular heartbeats.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds contain active compounds that contribute to the tea's sedative effects, helping regulate and stabilize the heart rate in cases of palpitation.
tea_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid that binds to benzodiazepine receptors in the brain, reducing anxiety and palpitations.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that possesses antioxidant properties, helping to reduce oxidative stress and irregular heartbeat.
Chamazulene: A chemical compound with anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
Alpha-bisabolol: A sesquiterpene that has calming and anti-spasmodic effects, helping to relax the heart muscle and reduce palpitations.
Matricin: A precursor to chamazulene, with anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, helping to reduce heart muscle spasms and irregular heartbeat.
tea_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to 212 degrees Fahrenheit, the optimal temperature for releasing chamomile's soothing properties.
Place one teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers or one chamomile tea bag in a heat-resistant container.
Pour the boiling water over the chamomile, allowing it to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the desired compounds.
Strain the liquid to remove the chamomile flowers, ensuring a smooth tea without sediment.
Sip the warm chamomile tea, slowly enjoying its calming effects to potentially alleviate the symptoms of palpitation.

teas.tea_desc: Valerian tea contains valerenic acid and other antioxidants known to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which can help decrease heart rate and minimize palpitations. This herbal tea works by increasing the amount of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, reducing excitability in the nervous system and promoting a calming effect that stabilizes heart rhythms. Furthermore, valerian tea has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, both of which often contribute to heart palpitations, by promoting relaxation and reducing tension. By decreasing overall tension and promoting mental and physical relaxation, consistent consumption of valerian tea can contribute to a significant reduction in palpitations. Lastly, its mild sedative properties aid in regulating the heart's rhythm, diminishing palpitations and fostering a sense of tranquility.
tea_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of antioxidants and is used to make medicinal tea for palpitation due to its calming and anti-arrhythmic properties.
Rhizomes: Rich in volatile oils, which help to regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Leaves: Contain valepotriates, which have been shown to have a relaxing effect on the heart and are used in medicinal tea for palpitation.
Flowers: High in flavonoids, which have been found to help regulate heart rhythm and are used to make medicinal tea for palpitation.
Seeds: Contain essential oils, which have a calming effect on the nervous system and are used to make medicinal tea for palpitation.
tea_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in valerian tea, such as valerianine, act as natural sedatives to help relax the heart and reduce palpitations.
Valepotriates: Valepotriates, including valtrate and isovaltrate, have anti-arrhythmic properties that help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
GABA: Gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in valerian tea inhibits nerve impulses in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing heart palpitations.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in valerian tea, such as luteolin and quercetin, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the heart and reduce palpitations.
Essential oil: The essential oil in valerian tea, including bornyl acetate and camphene, has calming and sedative effects that help reduce heart palpitations and promote relaxation.
tea_recipe: In a tea infuser, place 1 tablespoon of dried valerian root.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the infuser in a teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 10 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Remove the infuser to prevent over-steeping and bitterness.
Consume the tea, up to three times daily, for its calming effects on palpitations.

teas.tea_desc: Lemon balm tea contains anti-arrhythmic compounds that directly affect heart function, specifically rosmarinic acid. This acid inhibits the release of stress-inducing hormones, such as adrenaline, which can trigger palpitations. Rosmarinic acid also has a sedative effect on the heart, reducing the intensity and frequency of palpitations. Additionally, lemon balm possesses antioxidant properties that protect heart muscle cells from oxidative damage, promoting overall cardiovascular health. Furthermore, lemon balm tea has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote sleep, indirectly addressing a common cause of palpitations.
tea_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have calming properties that help regulate heart function and reduce palpitations.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant contain flavonoids that have a relaxing effect on the cardiovascular system, reducing palpitations and promoting normal heart rhythm.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a positive effect on heart function and can help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.

Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are a good source of essential oils and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby reducing palpitations.
tea_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant properties and helps reduce blood pressure.
Eugenol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, aiding in heart muscle relaxation.
Citral: Acts as a mild sedative, reducing heart rate and relieving symptoms of palpitation.
Nerol: Provides calming and soothing effects, helping to alleviate stress-induced palpitations.
Geraniol: Helps regulate heart rhythm, acting as a natural relaxant for the cardiovascular system.
tea_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried lemon balm leaves or 4 teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water and add the lemon balm leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day.

teas.tea_desc: Hawthorn tea aids in alleviating palpitations due to its cardiotonic properties, which strengthen and normalize the heart's contractions. This herbal tea contains active compounds, such as flavonoids and proanthocyanidins, that dilate coronary blood vessels, enhancing blood flow to the heart. Additionally, hawthorn tea acts as a natural calcium channel blocker, reducing the heart's oxygen demand and alleviating arrhythmias. Furthermore, it has antioxidant effects that protect heart cells from oxidative stress and damage. Lastly, hawthorn tea's positive inotropic and chronotropic effects support healthy heart function, thus minimizing palpitations.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and antioxidants, leaves help regulate heartbeat and alleviate irregular heart rhythms.
Flowers: Containing active compounds like vitexin and rutin, flowers offer calming effects on the heart and reduce palpitations.
Bark: Source of triterpene saponins, bark aids in treating heart-related conditions and controlling heart rate.
Seeds: Packed with amines and tannins, seeds help strengthen heart muscles, ultimately reducing palpitations.
Stems: Supplying various bioactive compounds, stems assist in regulating heart function and maintaining a steady heart rate.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidant compounds help strengthen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing palpitations.
Proanthocyanidins: Known for their cardiovascular benefits, they help lower blood pressure and reduce irregular heartbeats.
Oligomeric procyanidins: These powerful antioxidants support heart health by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can cause palpitations.
Vitexin: A flavonoid that has a positive inotropic effect, meaning it enhances the force of heartbeats, which can help regulate palpitations.
Epicatechins: These antioxidant flavonoids help protect heart cells from damage, maintaining a regular heart rhythm.
Hyperoside: A flavonoid with anti-arrhythmic properties, helping to maintain a normal heartbeat and alleviate palpitations.
tea_recipe: In a stove-top pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 teaspoons of dried hawthorn berries.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove hawthorn berries, discarding the solids.
Let the tea cool down to drinking temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of hawthorn tea daily, as needed for palpitation relief.

teas.tea_desc: Motherwort tea aids in alleviating palpitations due to its historical use as a cardiac tonic, recognized by its bitterness and warmth. This tea contains alkaloids, such as leonurine and stachydrine, that act directly on the heart, specifically the sinoatrial node, to regulate heartbeat rhythm. Furthermore, motherwort's antispasmodic properties help relax the smooth muscles around blood vessels, promoting blood flow and reducing palpitations caused by anxiety or stress. Additionally, this herbal tea's mild sedative effects soothe the nervous system, easing symptoms of rapid heart rate or irregular heartbeats linked to emotional imbalances. Ultimately, motherwort tea's combined actions on the heart, blood vessels, and nervous system work synergistically to manage palpitations effectively.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and tannins, leaves are widely used to treat palpitations due to their astringent and calming properties.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids, flowers help regulate heartbeat and offer anti-anxiety benefits, making them ideal for palpitation relief.
Stems: Packed with essential oils, stems contribute to calming the nervous system, reducing heart rate, and alleviating symptoms of palpitations.
Seeds: Abundant in alkaloids, seeds help relax the heart muscle, ease tension, and normalize heart rhythms, addressing palpitations effectively.
tea_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids in motherwort tea, such as stachydrine and caffeic acid, possesses anti-arrhythmic properties that help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitation.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin found in motherwort tea have cardioprotective effects, they help stabilize heart rhythm and alleviate symptoms of palpitation.
Tannins: Tannins in motherwort tea have astringent properties that help tone the heart muscles, reducing palpitations and irregular heartbeats.
Volatile oils: Volatile oils, including leonurine and stachyose, found in motherwort tea have sedative and hypotensive effects that can help regulate heart rate and reduce palpitation.
Triterpenes: Triterpenes like ursolic and oleanolic acid present in motherwort tea have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with palpitation.
tea_recipe: In a pot, place 2 teaspoons of dried motherwort herb.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to the pot and cover it.
Let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb pieces.
Drink the motherwort tea, up to three times a day, to help with palpitations.

teas.tea_desc: Skullcap tea contains scutellarin, a flavonoid that relaxes the heart muscle and slows down the heart rate, making it an effective remedy for palpitations. This flavonoid works by inhibiting calcium influx into heart muscle cells, reducing the force of heart contractions. Moreover, skullcap tea contains anti-anxiety and sedative properties attributed to its antioxidant and antispasmodic compounds, aiding in the management of stress-induced palpitations. Furthermore, skullcap tea promotes better sleep quality, enabling the body to maintain a regular heartbeat. In conclusion, skullcap tea's ability to reduce heart rate, inhibit calcium influx, promote relaxation, and improve sleep quality makes it a valuable aid in alleviating and preventing palpitations.
tea_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves of the skullcap tea plant help reduce oxidative stress, contributing to a balanced heart rate.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, skullcap flowers help soothe the nervous system, reducing palpitations.
Roots: With soothing alkaloids, the roots of the skullcap tea plant help relax the heart muscle, easing palpitations.
Stems: Packed with triterpene saponins, skullcap stems support cardiovascular health by promoting stable heart rhythms.
Seeds: Abundant in essential fatty acids, skullcap seeds help nourish the heart, supporting normal heart rates.
tea_constituents: Flavonoids: These antioxidants have a relaxant effect on blood vessels, reducing pressure on the heart and alleviating palpitations.
Melatonin: This hormone regulates sleep-wake cycles and can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can cause heart palpitations.
Tannins: These astringent compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce heart inflammation and related palpitations.
Saponins: These compounds have a regulatory effect on the heart, helping to normalize heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Potassium: This essential mineral helps regulate heart rhythms and can help reduce palpitations caused by potassium deficiency.
Magnesium: This mineral helps regulate heart rhythms and can help reduce palpitations caused by magnesium deficiency.
Calcium: This mineral is essential for heart function and can help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
tea_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb, crush it, and place in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the skullcap herb.
Steep the tea for 10-15 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the herb.
Drink the warm skullcap tea, slowly sipping it, for palpitation relief.

teas.tea_desc: Ashwagandha tea reduces palpitations by stabilizing the body's stress response. This adaptogenic herb contains withanolides, which possess anxiety-relieving and cardio-protective properties. By decreasing stress-induced hormonal fluctuations, ashwagandha tea helps regulate heart rhythms, diminishing irregular heartbeats and palpitations. Furthermore, it improves sleep quality and overall cardiovascular health, thus providing additional protection against heart-related issues. Ultimately, drinking ashwagandha tea helps alleviate palpitations by promoting a balanced nervous system and enhancing overall well-being.
tea_parts: Roots: The roots of ashwagandha tea plant are widely used in medicinal tea for palpitation due to their adaptogenic properties that help the body manage stress and anxiety, which are common causes of heart palpitations.
Leaves: The leaves of ashwagandha tea plant contain several active compounds, including withanolides, that have a calming effect on the nervous system and help regulate an irregular heartbeat.
Seeds: The seeds of ashwagandha tea plant are rich in antioxidants that protect the heart from oxidative stress and help reduce inflammation, which can contribute to palpitations.

Flowers: The flowers of ashwagandha tea plant contain several bioactive compounds that help regulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of heart palpitations.
tea_constituents: Withanolides: These constituents possess cardioprotective properties that help regulate heart beat and reduce palpitations.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in ashwagandha tea help regulate the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can cause palpitations.
Saponins: These constituents have a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce palpitations caused by stress or anxiety.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have antioxidant properties that improve heart health, reducing the risk of palpitations.
Steroidal lactones: These constituents have a sedative effect, helping to reduce palpitations caused by anxiety or stress.
Tannins: Tannins have astringent properties that help regulate heart beat and reduce palpitations.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water in a pot for 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of crushed ashwagandha root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles.
Serve hot and drink immediately for best results.

teas.tea_desc: Bacopa tea contains active compounds, namely bacosides, that exhibit anti-arrhythmic properties, according to scientific research. These bacosides regulate heart function by inhibiting abnormal contractions, thus reducing palpitations. Moreover, bacopa demonstrates adaptogenic qualities, supporting the body's response to stress, which may contribute to palpitation reduction. Additionally, this herbal tea acts as a natural cardio-tonic, promoting overall heart health by strengthening the heart muscle and improving blood circulation. Consequently, bacopa tea alleviates palpitations by addressing their underlying causes and enhancing cardiovascular well-being.
tea_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and alkaloids, the roots of bacopa tea plant are commonly used to treat irregular heartbeat, as they help regulate heart function and promote calmness.
Leaves: High in saponins and essential oils, bacopa leaves have a tranquilizing effect on the nervous system and are useful in strengthening the heart muscles, reducing palpitations.
Flowers: Packed with bioactive compounds, bacopa flowers aid in easing anxiety and stress, which can contribute to palpitations, promoting a steady heart rhythm.
Seeds: Containing beneficial compounds such as bacosides, bacopa seeds offer cardioprotective properties, helping to maintain a healthy heartbeat and reducing irregular heart rhythms.
tea_constituents: Bacosides: Alkaloids that have a relaxing effect on the heart, which can reduce palpitations.
Saponins: Plant compounds that have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate heartbeat and reduce palpitations.
Flavonoids: Antioxidants that have been shown to improve cardiovascular health, which can reduce the risk of developing palpitations.
Sterols: Plant compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation of the heart and reduce palpitations.
Alkaloids: Plant compounds that can have a sedative effect on the body, which can help reduce the symptoms of palpitations.
tea_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water, then add 1 tablespoon of dried bacopa leaves, reducing heat to a simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes, covered, to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the spent bacopa leaves.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid milk or other dairy products.
Drink the bacopa tea while warm, up to three times a day, to potentially alleviate symptoms of palpitation.

other_remedies: Among the most common and effective natural remedies for palpitations are deep breathing exercises, progressive muscle relaxation, and maintaining a healthy magnesium and potassium balance. Deep breathing exercises, which involve taking slow, deep breaths and exhaling slowly, help to activate the body's relaxation response, reducing the overall heart rate and alleviating symptoms of palpitations. Progressive muscle relaxation, on the other hand, entails tensing and then releasing different muscle groups in a systematic manner, promoting physical and mental relaxation. Ensuring an adequate intake of magnesium and potassium through diet or supplementation is also crucial for maintaining a regular heart rhythm. These natural remedies have been proven to be effective in managing palpitations, offering a safe and accessible alternative to pharmaceutical interventions.
causes: Stress and anxiety: These emotional states can cause an overstimulation of the heart, leading to heart palpitations.
Exercise: Physical activity increases heart rate, and in some cases, it can cause heart palpitations, especially during intense workouts or when starting an exercise routine.
Caffeine intake: Consuming caffeine from sources like coffee, energy drinks, or chocolate can trigger heart palpitations by stimulating the heart.
Nicotine use: Smoking or using nicotine products can cause heart palpitations as nicotine is a stimulant that increases heart rate.
Hormonal changes: Fluctuations in hormone levels, such as those experienced during pregnancy, menstruation, or menopause, can cause heart palpitations.
Underlying heart conditions: Conditions such as arrhythmias, heart valve problems, or congenital heart diseases can result in heart palpitations.
Electrolyte imbalances: Imbalances in electrolytes like potassium, magnesium, or calcium can affect heart function and cause palpitations.
Low blood sugar: Hypoglycemia, or low blood sugar levels, can trigger heart palpitations as the body tries to compensate for the lack of energy.
Medications: Certain medications, such as those used to treat asthma, thyroid conditions, or high blood pressure, can cause heart palpitations as a side effect.
Illness or infection: Fever, infections, or other medical conditions can cause heart palpitations as the body responds to the illness.
definition: Palpitation refers to the conscious sensation of one's heart beating in an abnormal or irregular way. This condition impacts people's lives by causing discomfort, distress, and anxiety. The unpredictable and sometimes rapid heartbeats can interfere with daily activities, disrupt sleep patterns, and even trigger panic attacks. As a result, individuals experiencing palpitations often experience reduced quality of life and may seek medical attention to alleviate their symptoms.
